etymology.description: phonetic 冎(冎) (guā, "jawbone") + semantic 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") = 咼(咼) -> sound with semantic element
etymology.interpretation: The traditional character 咼(咼) (guō, "pot") combines the phonetic element 冎(冎) (guā, "jawbone") with the semantic element 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth"), suggesting a mouth-like opening of a vessel or pot.
etymology.simplification: 咼(咼) was simplified to 呙(呙) by replacing 冎(冎) (guā, "jawbone") with 冂(冂) (jiōng, "open enclosure") and changing the internal structure.
